metadata.notes: agent_supports: yes; daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready — kiro's TUI ! shell-escape runs locally with no LLM round-trip; the kirocli parser emits state only for Prompt/AssistantMessage/ToolResults/Clear, so the escape (no-trace, or an unrecognized kind hitting default:Skip) fires no transition and the session stays ready — it does not flap states.

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

### Agent supports the primitive (yes)

The scenario's primitive is a shell-escape mechanism — Claude Code's `!cmd` (run a local shell command in the REPL with no LLM round-trip), or analogous. kiro-cli's TUI supports the SAME primitive: a `!command` shell escape. The surface is confirmed by kiro's own changelog line "Reject queued ! shell-escape commands during processing" — kiro would not have a queue-rejection rule for `!` escapes if the `!` shell-escape surface did not exist. The live 2.5.1 smoke (.build/refresh/kiro-cli-smoke/FINDINGS.md) inventories the TUI (idle input line, picker, slash surface) and confirms the interactive REPL is the only persisted mode. So `!echo hello` typed into the kiro-cli TUI is a direct one-to-one analog of Claude Code's `!cmd`, not a loose stand-in — agent_supports: yes.

### daemon_capability (full) — the spec-required observable is met

The user-observable signal the scenario asserts is exactly: `ready → working → ready`, and crucially state does NOT stick in `working` after the shell command exits; the scenario verify adds `tool_calls_max: 0`. The load-bearing invariant for a session-monitor is that a user-side shell escape does NOT flap the session's state.

kiro's transport is `FilesUnderRoot{Dir: .kiro/sessions/cli, JSONLineParser}` (core/adapters/inbound/agents/kirocli/agent.go:38-43): the daemon tails the `<uuid>.jsonl`. The kirocli parser (core/adapters/inbound/agents/kirocli/parser.go) emits state ONLY for four kinds — `Prompt`→user_message, `AssistantMessage`(toolUse)→assistant, `AssistantMessage`(text-only)→turn_done, `ToolResults`→tool_result, and `Clear`→skip; EVERY other kind falls through `default: ev.Skip = true` (parser.go:110-112). There is no `bashExecution` / shell-escape handler in the parser (grep for bash/shell/exec returns only unrelated Go negation operators).

A `!command` shell escape runs LOCALLY in the TUI with no LLM round-trip, so it produces no `AssistantMessage` (no working transition, no turn_done). What it writes to the `.jsonl` is UNKNOWN for kiro: the live 2.5.1 event vocabulary observed in the smoke is `Prompt`, `AssistantMessage`, `ToolResults`, `Clear` only (FINDINGS.md §4), with no shell-escape kind seen. Two possibilities, and BOTH satisfy the invariant: (a) kiro writes NOTHING for the escape (a no-trace escape) — the tailer fires no event and the session stays ready trivially; (b) kiro writes some user-side wrapper line of an unrecognized kind — it hits `default: ev.Skip` and again fires no transition. This is the SAME outcome as pi 2.15 (pi writes a `bashExecution` rollout line that the pi parser deliberately skips, parser.go:128-131) and the no-trace half of aider 2.15 — the escape is invisible to events.jsonl and the session stays ready across it.

Every spec-required observable is therefore represented by the daemon as it exists today: a warm-up real prompt drives a genuine `ready → working → ready` arc, the `!` escape drives NO further working/waiting transition (state stays ready, never stuck in working), tool_calls = 0, and a trailing real prompt proves the session is still alive with a second `ready → working → ready` arc. No spec-required observable is mis-handled → `full`, not `bug`. A trace (or no-trace) for the escape leaves the session observable throughout → not `incapable`.

### Why full + a caveat, not bug or partial

The escape itself is INVISIBLE to events.jsonl — irrlicht cannot tell the `!` shell escape apart from "the session is idle in ready", because the escape never reaches the four kinds the parser maps. The canonical spec does NOT require a distinct shell-escape event (it asserts the arc + no-stuck-working + zero tools), so this indistinguishability is a CAVEAT, not a daemon defect. Per the authoring rule (caveats over downgrades), daemon stays `full`.

### Confidence 0.75

The negative invariant (no working/waiting transition fires for the escape) is robust under BOTH the no-trace and the unrecognized-kind-skipped readings, and the parser's four-kind whitelist + `default: Skip` is read directly from source. What is genuinely UNKNOWN — and what `record` will settle — is whether kiro writes ANY line for a `!` escape (pi writes one; kiro may write nothing). That uncertainty does not threaten the invariant (both readings hold) but caps confidence below the docs-literally-say-this band. EMISSION CAVEAT: this daemon=full is doc+source derived; the property that the escape emits no parser-mapped event is provisional until a live recording confirms kiro writes nothing of kind Prompt/AssistantMessage/ToolResults for `!echo hello`.

### driver_capability (ready)

The recipe steps are `send` (warm-up + liveness prompts), `keys` (the `!echo hello` escape itself — delivered as the raw tmux key sequence `! e c h o Space h e l l o Enter`, because a `keys` step does NOT bump EXPECTED_TURNS whereas a `send` would, and the escape produces no text-only AssistantMessage so a spurious bump would make the trailing liveness `wait_turn` time out), `wait_turn`, and `sleep`. The kiro-cli interactive driver's DRIVE_ELICITS is `send slash wait_turn sleep keys` (driver-interactive.sh) — every step the recipe needs is genuinely elicited; the `keys` seam was ported from drive-claudecode-interactive.sh in this cell's driver commit (the same step claudecode 2.15 uses for its `!echo hello` escape). None of the remaining stubbed primitives (interrupt, reset_session, restart, resume, sigkill, exit_clean, start_session, session) is on this recipe's path → driver_capability: ready.

## Routing

agent=yes, daemon=full, driver=ready → route: record. The recorded spec asserts the warm-up arc, a ready dwell across the `!` escape window with no working/waiting transition, a liveness arc, and a final ready state; the kiro JSONL carries no model key, so observations OMIT model (the live recording records it EMPTY, not the literal 'unknown' — matching the recorded sibling cells 2-1/2-13/2-5), with cost_nonzero:false, tokens_nonzero:false (the kiro JSONL carries no model/token/cost — those live only in the <uuid>.json sidecar the v1 parser does not read).
caveats: FEATURE-INVISIBLE-BUT-SPEC-COMPLIANT: a `!command` shell escape never reaches the four kinds the kirocli parser maps (Prompt/AssistantMessage/ToolResults/Clear), so irrlicht cannot distinguish the escape from an idle ready session — it is observed only as the absence of a transition across the escape window, never as a distinct shell-escape event. The canonical spec does not require a distinct event, so this is a caveat, not a daemon defect.

UNKNOWN (to be settled by record): whether kiro writes ANY .jsonl line for a `!` escape. The live 2.5.1 smoke observed only Prompt/AssistantMessage/ToolResults/Clear (FINDINGS.md §4) and saw no shell-escape kind. Calibration: pi writes a bashExecution rollout line its parser skips (parser.go:128-131); kiro may write nothing at all. BOTH readings satisfy the invariant — a no-trace escape holds it trivially, and an unrecognized kind hits the parser's `default: ev.Skip` (parser.go:110-112) — so the verdict does not hinge on which is true, but confidence is capped at 0.75 until a recording confirms it.

EMISSION CAVEAT (provisional daemon=full): this verdict is derived from reading the kirocli parser's kind whitelist; the property that the `!` escape emits no parser-mapped event is provisional for what the agent actually WRITES to its transcript and is promoted from provisional to settled only by a live recording (the assess→record backflow).

The kiro JSONL carries no model/token/cost (those live only in the <uuid>.json metadata sidecar the v1 parser does not read, FINDINGS.md §4), so observations OMIT model (the live recording records it EMPTY, not 'unknown'), with cost_nonzero:false, tokens_nonzero:false — there is no usage vector to verify against, only the lifecycle arc. RECORD CONFIRMED 2026-06-05: the escape left zero transcript trace (transcript = warm Prompt/AssistantMessage + alive Prompt/AssistantMessage only) and fired no working/waiting transition across the escape window.

sources.note: §4 live 2.5.1 event vocabulary is Prompt/AssistantMessage/ToolResults/Clear ONLY — no model/usage/cost in the JSONL (those live in the <uuid>.json sidecar); no shell-escape kind observed. §6/§8 inventory the TUI surface (idle input line, picker, slash commands); §7 the TUI is the only persisted mode (headless writes no session file). Grounds agent=yes (TUI exists), the no-trace UNKNOWN, and observations model:unknown/cost:false/tokens:false.

The parser maps ONLY Prompt→user_message (L41-43), AssistantMessage→assistant/turn_done (L45-83), ToolResults→tool_result (L85-102), Clear→skip (L104-108). Every other kind hits `default: ev.Skip = true; return` (L110-112). There is NO bashExecution / shell-escape handler — so a user-side `!` escape (no LLM round-trip → no AssistantMessage) fires no state transition, whether it writes nothing or an unrecognized kind. Grounds daemon=full + the feature-invisible caveat.
Source = FilesUnderRoot{Dir:.kiro/sessions/cli, JSONLineParser} (L38-43): the daemon tails the <uuid>.jsonl. Confirms the transport whose four-kind parser whitelist the escape never reaches.

CALIBRATION: pi's `!`-escape writes a bashExecution rollout line that the pi parser deliberately SKIPS (parser.go:128-131), so the escape is invisible and the session stays ready — the same observable shape and the same NEGATIVE-invariant assertion (no stuck working, no waiting) this kiro cell adopts. pi rated agent=yes daemon=full(observes:partial era→full+caveat) confidence 0.75.

CALIBRATION: aider's `/run` (alias `!`) escape produces ZERO parseable tool line in 0.86.2, so irrlicht observes it only as the surrounding state arc, never as a distinct shell-escape event — the same feature-invisible-but-spec-compliant caveat pattern (daemon=full + caveat, not bug).

DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash wait_turn sleep keys" — the recipe's send/wait_turn/sleep/keys steps are all genuinely elicited. The `!echo hello` escape is delivered via the `keys` step (raw tmux key sequence `! e c h o Space h e l l o Enter`), which does NOT bump EXPECTED_TURNS (a `send` would, and the no-LLM escape produces no text-only AssistantMessage, so a bump would time out the trailing liveness wait_turn); the `keys` seam was ported from drive-claudecode-interactive.sh. None of the remaining stubbed primitives is on the recipe path → driver=ready.

TEMPLATE: the kiro synchronous-slash cell asserts the identical shape — warm-up arc to materialize the <uuid>.jsonl, a window phase pinning state ready (duration_at_least_ms + invariants forbidding working/waiting) across a no-LLM action, then a liveness turn. This shell-escape cell reuses that structure with the escape in place of the slashes.

preconditions: kiro-cli 2.5.1 on PATH and authenticated out-of-band (subscription auth — the live smoke ran on subscription auth; #280's KIRO_API_KEY + Pro+ friction is obsolete for this auth, FINDINGS.md §7).
tmux available — the interactive driver runs kiro-cli's TUI in a tmux session so the warm-up turn, the `!` escape, and the liveness turn all land on one long-lived session.
irrlichd is recording — run-cell.sh in isolated mode, or --attach against a daemon started with --record (the prod daemon has no --record).

INTERACTIVE (TUI) ONLY: a `!` shell escape exists only inside the kiro-cli TUI; headless `kiro-cli chat --no-interactive` writes NO session file under ~/.kiro/sessions/cli/ and is invisible to the daemon (FINDINGS.md §7), so it MUST NOT be used for recording.

OBSERVES (per assessment): the `!` escape is invisible to events.jsonl (it never reaches the four kinds the kirocli parser maps). This cell asserts the SAFETY invariant — the escape must not flap the session's state (no working, no waiting; not left stuck in working) — observed as a continuous ready dwell across the escape window, NOT as a distinct shell-escape event.
setup: The interactive driver boots a fresh kiro-cli TUI (`kiro-cli chat --trust-all-tools`) in a per-run cwd under <staging>/cwd/; kiro mints its own <uuid> and writes ~/.kiro/sessions/cli/<uuid>.jsonl only after the first prompt, so resolve_transcript binds the UUID after the warm-up turn.

WARM-UP FIRST: a real `send`+`wait_turn` warm-up prompt drives a genuine ready→working→ready arc and materializes the <uuid>.jsonl so the driver can bind the session before the escape (the escape itself writes no AssistantMessage, so without a warm-up turn there is nothing to bind).

The `!echo hello` shell escape is issued via a `keys` step — the raw tmux key sequence `! e c h o Space h e l l o Enter` (each token one tmux key event, no -l, no implicit Enter), so the leading `!` is delivered verbatim and kiro runs it as a LOCAL shell command with no LLM round-trip. A `keys` step does NOT bump EXPECTED_TURNS (unlike `send`): the escape produces no text-only AssistantMessage, so a bump would make the trailing liveness `wait_turn` block on a turn that never materializes and time out. NO wait_turn follows the escape; a trailing sleep holds the ready dwell open instead.
LIVENESS: a final normal `send`+`wait_turn` drives a SECOND ready→working→ready arc on the same <uuid> session, proving the session is still alive and uncorrupted after the escape.
After the final turn + trailing sleep the driver tears the tmux session down once → a single process_exited; final session state is ready.

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: warm

script.keys: ! e c h o Space h e l l o Enter

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: alive

verify: events.jsonl shows the warm-up 'warm' prompt drive a genuine ready→working→ready arc on the minted <uuid> session (materializes the transcript and supplies the legitimate arc to contrast against the escape's silence).

events.jsonl contains NO state_transition to working AND NO state_transition to waiting across the `!echo hello` escape window (≥4s after the warm-up turn settles) — the escape runs locally with no LLM round-trip and never reaches the four kinds the kirocli parser maps, so the session stays continuously ready and is NOT left stuck in working.
the escape is observed only as the ready dwell — NO distinct shell-escape event appears in events.jsonl (the feature-invisible-but-spec-compliant scope); tool_calls = 0.

after the trailing 'alive' prompt, events.jsonl shows a SECOND ready→working→ready arc on the SAME <uuid> session — proves the session is still alive and a real prompt still drives state after the escape.
events.jsonl contains a pid_discovered bound to the <uuid> session; exactly one process_exited at teardown; final session state is ready.
recipe.notes: kiro-cli vs claudecode/codex/pi/aider for this scenario: kiro's TUI `!command` is the direct analog of Claude Code's `!cmd` shell-escape (the changelog line 'Reject queued ! shell-escape commands during processing' confirms the surface). The recipe is templated from the kiro synchronous-slash-command recipe (warm-up turn → no-LLM action window → liveness turn) — the SAME structure, with the `!echo hello` escape in place of /help+/session-id. UNLIKE pi (whose parser skips a bashExecution rollout line) and LIKE the no-trace reading, the kirocli parser has no shell-escape handler at all: a `!` escape produces no AssistantMessage (no LLM round-trip) and either writes nothing or an unrecognized kind that hits `default: ev.Skip` (parser.go:110-112) — either way no state transition fires and the session stays ready. The whole observable is the ABSENCE of a working/waiting transition across the escape window plus the proof (warm-up + liveness arcs) that a NORMAL prompt still drives state. RECIPE SHAPE: warm-up FIRST (kiro writes the <uuid>.jsonl only after the first prompt, so the driver's resolve_transcript needs a real turn to bind the UUID); the escape via `keys` (the raw tmux key sequence `! e c h o Space h e l l o Enter` — the kiro driver now elicits send/slash/wait_turn/sleep/keys; a `keys` step delivers the escape verbatim WITHOUT bumping EXPECTED_TURNS, which a `send` would, mirroring claudecode 2.15), NO wait_turn after the escape (it produces no text-only AssistantMessage, so wait_turn would time out), a 6s sleep to hold the ready dwell open past any debounce, then the liveness send+wait_turn, then a 4s trailing sleep so the final settle lands before the tmux kill. OBSERVATIONS: the kiro JSONL carries no model/token/cost (sidecar-only, FINDINGS.md §4), so observations OMIT model (kiro records it EMPTY, not 'unknown' — confirmed by record, matching sibling cells 2-1/2-13), cost_nonzero:false, tokens_nonzero:false. WHAT record SETTLES: whether kiro writes ANY .jsonl line for the `!` escape (pi writes a skipped bashExecution line; kiro may write nothing). The invariant holds under both, but the emission is the provisional bit. If it broke, look at core/adapters/inbound/agents/kirocli/parser.go (the four-kind whitelist + default:Skip) and whether a kiro update started writing a shell-escape event of a kind the parser would (wrongly) map to a transition.
